title: Bridge Essay: Brain mechanisms in religion and spirituality: An integrative predictive processing framework.
concept: Social Incarnation — intersubjectivity, embodied empathy, social predictive processing.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Social Incarnation** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Social Incarnation — intersubjectivity, embodied empathy, social predictive processing.

**Brain mechanisms in religion and spirituality: An integrative predictive processing framework.** - Neuroscience and biobehavioral reviews (2017). DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2016.12.031. PMID: 28041787. We present the theory of predictive processing as a unifying framework to account for the neurocognitive basis of religion and spirituality. Our model is substantiated by discussing four different brain mechanisms that play a key role in religion and spirituality: temporal brain areas are associated with religious visions and ecstatic experiences; multisensory brain areas and the default mode network are involved in self-transcendent experiences; the Theory of Mind-network is associated with pra
